Bay Area Discounts

College & University Student Resources

Comprehensive guide to free and discounted benefits available exclusively to students enrolled at Bay Area colleges and universities. Benefits include transit passes, food assistance, emergency grants, recreation access, and more. Always verify benefits and eligibility with your college or university as programs and requirements may change.

Quick Navigation:

  • Discount Platforms
  • SparkPoint
  • Community Colleges
  • California State Universities
  • University of California
  • Private Universities

Discount Platforms

These platforms verify student status and connect you with discounts from many different brands. Offers change often, so always confirm details on each site before purchasing.

Service Benefit How to Use It Timeframe Link
Student Beans Free student verification and access to promo codes and special pricing at hundreds of brands including clothing, tech, and food delivery Create a free account, verify enrollment with your school email or documentation, then browse offers and use the codes or links at participating stores Ongoing Student Beans
UNiDAYS Central hub for student discounts and limited-time offers from major brands such as Apple, Adidas, Costco promos, and more Sign up for a free account, verify student status, then use in-app codes online or show your UNiDAYS ID in participating stores Ongoing UNiDAYS
ID.me Student Single login that verifies student status and unlocks student pricing at many retailers and services that use ID.me Create a free ID.me account, complete student verification, then look for “Verify with ID.me” buttons at partner websites and follow the prompts Ongoing ID.me Student
SheerID Verification gateway used by many brands for student-only deals on streaming, clothing, software, and more When a brand uses SheerID, complete the short verification form with your school details or upload documentation, then redeem the offer on the brand’s site Ongoing SheerID Student Deals

SparkPoint

SparkPoint Centers by United Ways Bay Area (UWBA) provides free, confidential, and judgement-free support to help student families build lasting financial stability and access public benefits. Their centers offer coaching and connections across four key areas (services provided may vary by center):

  1. Financial stability: Coaching, budgeting, credit repair, and tax help.
  2. Employment & education: Job search, skills training, and career growth.
  3. Food & housing security: Access to food, rental support, transit, and benefits enrollment.
  4. Long-term goals: Increase income, improve credit, grow savings, and reduce debt.
College/University City Link
Cañada College Redwood City Cañada College
Chabot College Hayward Chabot College
City College of San Francisco (CCSF) San Francisco CCSF
College of San Mateo San Mateo CSM
Contra Costa College San Pablo CCC
Diablo Valley College Oakland DVC
Laney College Pleasant Hill Laney College
San Francisco State University (SFSU) San Francisco SFSU
Skyline College San Bruno Skyline College
Solano Community College Fairfield SparkPoint Solano

Community Colleges

Jump to:

  • Chabot-Las Positas Community College District
  • City College of San Francisco
  • College of Marin
  • Contra Costa Community College District
  • Foothill-De Anza Community College District
  • Napa Valley College
  • Ohlone Community College District
  • Peralta Community College District
  • San Jose-Evergreen Community College District
  • San Mateo County Community College District
  • Santa Rosa Junior College

Chabot-Las Positas Community College District

Colleges: Chabot College (Hayward), Las Positas College (Livermore)

Program College/University Benefit How to Use It Timeframe Link
Emergency Grants Chabot, Las Positas Up to $500 for books, supplies, food, and transportation Apply through Basic Needs office Academic year Chabot Basic Needs
Food Pantry Chabot FRESH Market: student-led pantry with groceries, clothing, hygiene products, other basic necessities, and CalFresh assistance Visit pantry with student ID Academic year FRESH Market
Food Resources Las Positas List of available food resources for students Visit resource guide Ongoing Food Resources
Promise Program Chabot, Las Positas 2 years free tuition for first-time full-time students, no income requirement Apply as incoming student First-time college students College Promise

City College of San Francisco

Program College/University Benefit How to Use It Timeframe Link
Free City Grant CCSF Full tuition waiver for San Francisco residents Enroll at CCSF and apply Academic year Free City
Library Technology Lending CCSF Free laptop/chromebook, WiFi hotspot, and calculator loans (one of each per student, semester-long) Check out from library Academic year Library Technology

College of Marin

Program College/University Benefit How to Use It Timeframe Link
Basic Needs College of Marin Support with everyday necessities, including financial support, food, health services, housing, technology, and transportation Apply through Student Services Academic year Financial Aid
COM Cupboard College of Marin Weekly produce from SF-Marin Food Bank Visit cupboard Academic year Student Services
Emergency Ride Home College of Marin Receive a reimbursement for eligible emergency rides home who normally commute by any means other than driving alone (up to $125/ride; capped at $500/year) Apply for reimbursement online Ongoing Marin Transit
Free Marin Transit College of Marin Free unlimited rides on all Marin Transit routes with student ID Show student ID when boarding Academic year Marin Transit

Contra Costa Community College District

Colleges: Contra Costa College (San Pablo), Diablo Valley College (Pleasant Hill), Los Medanos College (Pittsburg)

Program College/University Benefit How to Use It Timeframe Link
511 Emergency Rides CCC, DVC, LMC 6 free emergency Lyft/Uber rides annually Register through 511 Contra Costa Academic year 511 Contra Costa
511 Transit Incentives CCC, DVC, LMC Free $25 Clipper card for taking transit pledge Complete transit pledge online Academic year 511 Contra Costa
Full-Time Free Tuition CCC, DVC, LMC Automatic fee waivers for 2 years (~$1,104/year), no income requirement Submit FAFSA or CA Dream Act and enroll full-time 2 years 4CD Free Tuition
Technology Loans DVC Free semester-long Chromebook, Wi-Fi hotspot, and calculator rentals Check out from library Academic semester DVC Technology
TimelyCare CCC, DVC, LMC Free 24/7 telehealth for medical and mental health Access through student portal Academic year TimelyCare
Wrap-Around Emergency Fund CCC Up to $500 emergency grants disbursed within one week, no immigration status requirements Apply through Basic Needs Academic year CCC Basic Needs

Foothill-De Anza Community College District

Colleges: De Anza College (Cupertino), Foothill College (Los Altos Hills)

Program College/University Benefit How to Use It Timeframe Link
Dental Hygiene Clinic Foothill Free teeth cleaning and X-rays for students who paid student body fees (worth hundreds of dollars) Schedule appointment with clinic Academic year Dental Hygiene
Emergency Grants De Anza One-time cash grants up to $750 for students facing financial hardship or urgent expenses Submit completed PDF application to dabasicneeds@deanza.edu Academic year Emergency Grants
Free Bicycle Rentals De Anza Free bicycle rentals for students 18+ with DASG ID cards, renewable quarterly Apply for DASG bike program Academic quarters DASG Bike Program
Free Legal Advice De Anza Free 30-minute legal consultations via Zoom for DASG cardholders Complete online appointment request form Academic quarters Legal Advice
Owls Nest Foothill Free $50 Walmart e-Gift Cards for registered students, free snacks, weekly groceries, hygiene products Visit Owls Nest Academic year Owls Nest
Promise Programs De Anza, Foothill Two years free tuition and fees for first-time students Apply as incoming student First-time students De Anza Promise
Roary’s Market De Anza Free groceries, meat, produce, hot meals (Wednesdays), clothing, Crumbl cookies while supplies last Visit with DASG student card Academic year Roary’s Market
VTA SmartPass De Anza, Foothill $7.75/quarter for unlimited VTA bus and light rail (students enrolled in 12+ units) Purchase through student portal Academic quarters VTA SmartPass

Napa Valley College

Program College/University Benefit How to Use It Timeframe Link
Basic Needs Center Napa Valley College 2-3 bags of groceries monthly Visit Basic Needs Center Academic year Basic Needs
Emergency Grants Napa Valley College Up to $1,500 (Spring) and $1,000 (Summer), no FAFSA required Apply through Financial Aid Spring/Summer Financial Aid
Promise Scholarship Napa Valley College Covers enrollment fees, lab fees, material fees, reduced-cost parking permits Apply for Promise Scholarship Academic year NVC Promise

Ohlone Community College District

College: Ohlone College (Fremont/Newark)

Program College/University Benefit How to Use It Timeframe Link
Food Pantry Ohlone Pop-up food pantries (served ~529 students/week), CalFresh assistance provides up to $281/month Visit pantry Academic year Basic Needs
Ohlone Promise Scholarship Ohlone Full tuition, books, and fees for two years (up to 60 units) for Tri-City area high school seniors maintaining 2.0 GPA Apply as graduating senior High school seniors Ohlone Promise
Tutoring Centers Ohlone Biology & Chemistry Learning Center, ASL/Deaf Studies Lab with specialized drop-in tutoring Drop in during center hours Academic year Tutoring

Peralta Community College District

Colleges: College of Alameda, Berkeley City College (Berkeley), Laney College (Oakland), Merritt College (Oakland)

Program College/University Benefit How to Use It Timeframe Link
AC Transit EasyPass College of Alameda, BCC, Laney, Merritt Unlimited bus rides on all AC Transit routes for $47.32/semester (~$700+ annual value); 6+ units required Purchase through college Academic semester Peralta Transit
College of Alameda Promise College of Alameda 2 years free tuition + book stipends for first-time full-time students Apply as incoming student First-time students COA Promise
District Health Clinic Laney College Free primary care and mental health for all district students paying $18 health fee Visit clinic with student ID Academic year Health Services
East Bay Community Law Center All Peralta colleges Free immigration, housing, eviction defense through UCRC partnerships Schedule appointment Academic year Legal Services
Emergency Grants College of Alameda $300 Safeway gift cards, $100 gas/Clipper cards; multiple grants available per semester Apply through Basic Needs Academic year COA Basic Needs
Food Pantries College of Alameda, BCC, Laney, Merritt Campus food pantries at all four colleges Visit pantry with student ID Academic year Basic Needs
Free Daily Meals College of Alameda Free daily breakfast and lunch Visit cafeteria Academic year COA Food

San Jose-Evergreen Community College District

Colleges: Evergreen Valley College (San Jose), San Jose City College

Program College/University Benefit How to Use It Timeframe Link
ASG Emergency Grants EVC, SJCC Emergency funding for financial/housing crises, once per calendar year Apply through Associated Students Academic year Student Services
First 2 Free Promise EVC, SJCC Covers enrollment fees ($690/semester for 15 units) plus $500 Books and Supplies Grant Apply for Promise program First 2 years First 2 Free
F.R.E.S.H. Market EVC, SJCC Campus pantry and bi-monthly drive-through distributions (1st and 3rd Fridays) with Second Harvest Visit pantry or attend distribution Academic year Basic Needs

San Mateo County Community College District

Colleges: Cañada College (Redwood City), College of San Mateo, Skyline College (San Bruno)

Program College/University Benefit How to Use It Timeframe Link
Campus Dining Card Cañada, CSM, Skyline $100/month dining card for food-insecure students Apply through SparkPoint Academic year SMCCD Dining Card Application
Finish Line Fund Cañada, CSM, Skyline Foundation emergency grants averaging $1,000/student; over $2 million distributed annually Apply through Financial Aid Academic year Foundation
Promise Scholars Program Cañada, CSM, Skyline Up to 3 years coverage: tuition + $750/year book vouchers + priority enrollment + dedicated counseling Apply for Promise Scholars Up to 3 years Promise Scholars
SamTrans Way2Go Pass Cañada, CSM, Skyline FREE unlimited bus pass (5,000 distributed annually); Level 2 students receive $50/month transit support Apply for pass Academic year SamTrans

Santa Rosa Junior College

Program College/University Benefit How to Use It Timeframe Link
Bear Cub Basics SRJC $15 meal vouchers (4 per semester) Request through Basic Needs Per semester Basic Needs
Clipper BayPass (Pilot) SRJC Unlimited rides on ALL 24 Bay Area transit agencies (BART, Muni, Caltrain, SMART, ferries) Register for pilot program Through June 2025 BayPass FAQ
Free Transit SRJC Free Sonoma County Transit, Santa Rosa CityBus, and Petaluma Transit year-round with CubCard Show CubCard when boarding Academic year Transportation
SRJC for Free SRJC $600-850/semester for students taking 12+ units Apply for program Academic year SRJC for Free

West Valley-Mission Community College District

Colleges: Mission College (Santa Clara), West Valley College (Saratoga)

Program College/University Benefit How to Use It Timeframe Link
AB 3158 Free Tuition Mission, West Valley 100% free tuition for ALL district residents—no income requirement, no complicated forms Be a district resident and enroll Ongoing Free Community College
Free Cafeteria Meals Mission, West Valley $10.8 million program providing free cafeteria meals to students taking 6+ in-person units Visit cafeteria with student ID Academic year Mission News
Free Parking & Services Mission, West Valley Free parking, free health services, free technology loans Access with student ID Academic year Student Services
Mission Promise Mission Up to 3 years free tuition + books with counseling support Apply for Mission Promise Up to 3 years Mission Promise
Office of Student Needs West Valley Free clothing, haircuts, lactation rooms Visit Office of Student Needs Academic year Student Needs
VTA Transit Cards Mission, West Valley Free VTA Clipper cards and Lyft vouchers Request through Student Services Academic year Transportation
WVC Community Grant West Valley Up to $3,000/year for textbooks, transportation, and expenses Apply for Community Grant Academic year Financial Aid

California State Universities (CSU)

Jump to:

  • CSU East Bay
  • CSU Maritime Academy
  • San Francisco State University
  • San Jose State University
  • Sonoma State University

CSU East Bay

Program Benefit How to Use It Timeframe Link
AC Transit EasyPass Unlimited rides on all AC Transit routes with personalized photo Clipper card Receive at orientation Academic year (launched Fall 2025) EasyPass
Free Equipment Rentals Free same-day sports and fitness equipment checkout at RAW Center Present Bay Card at rental counter Academic year RAW Center
Pioneers for H.O.P.E. Comprehensive emergency assistance: temporary housing, bill payment help, food pantry with fresh produce, meat, and snacks Visit H.O.P.E. office Academic year H.O.P.E.
Recreation & Wellness Center 54,000 sq ft facility with two-story fitness center, elevated jogging track, and group fitness classes (worth $500+ per year vs commercial gyms) Access with Bay Card Academic year RAW Center

CSU Maritime Academy

Program Benefit How to Use It Timeframe Link
Coast Guard Licenses Third Mate or Third Assistant Engineer licenses (internationally recognized credentials) for applicable programs Complete required program Upon graduation Academics
Recreation & Aquatic Center NCAA-competition gymnasium and 50-meter pool with wave machine that simulates storm conditions, available for recreation Access with student ID Academic year PEAC
Western Undergraduate Exchange (WUE) Tuition at 150 percent of in-state rates for students from 15 western states (saves thousands vs out-of-state tuition) Apply as WUE-eligible student Academic year WUE

San Francisco State University

Program Benefit How to Use It Timeframe Link
AS Food Pantry Free groceries serving 700 to 800 students weekly, no proof of financial need required Walk in with student ID Academic year Gator Groceries
Free Adobe Creative Cloud Free Creative Cloud for all matriculated students (Adobe Creative Campus) regardless of major Download via student portal Academic year SFSU Academic Technology
Gator Grub Alert App notifications about free food events on campus Download app Academic year Basic Needs
Gator Pass $130 per semester for unlimited rides on all 22 Bay Area transit agencies (Muni, BART, Caltrain, ferries, VTA, and more) Auto-loaded on student One Card Fall and Spring semesters Gator Pass
Immigrant Legal Defense Free immigration legal services, including DACA renewals and family petitions Schedule appointment Academic year Legal Services
Mashouf Wellness Center 41-foot climbing wall, pools, hot tub, sauna, unlimited group fitness ($178 per semester in fees) Access with student ID Academic year Recreation
Zen Den Safe sleep space for housing-insecure students plus Basic Needs Lounges for extreme commuters and car-sleepers Contact Basic Needs office Academic year Basic Needs

San Jose State University

Program Benefit How to Use It Timeframe Link
Emergency Housing Up to 28 days of emergency housing through Bill Wilson Center partnership, plus crisis grants Contact SJSU Cares case manager As needed SJSU Cares
Free Professional Clothing Free career clothing through Office of Sustainability partnership Visit clothing resource Academic year SJSU Cares
Spartan Food Pantry Free groceries with more than 33,000 visits annually, no appointment or income proof required Walk in with Tower Card Academic year Spartan Food Pantry
Spartan Recreation Center 128,000 sq ft facility with two 50-meter pools, climbing wall, and indoor track included in student fees Access with Tower Card Academic year SRAC

Sonoma State University

Program Benefit How to Use It Timeframe Link
CalFresh Support Application assistance for up to $292 per month in grocery benefits Visit Noma Cares Central Academic year Basic Needs
Career Closet Free professional attire for interviews Visit career services Academic year Basic Needs
Emergency Basic Needs Grants Grants for students facing unforeseen crises Apply through Noma Cares Central Academic year BNI Program
Emergency Temporary Housing Free short-term on-campus housing up to 30 days for students experiencing homelessness Contact Housing Resources As needed Housing Resources
Lobo’s Pantry Free groceries and hygiene products including diapers and menstrual products Visit pantry Academic year Basic Needs
Outdoor Resource Center Camping and outdoor equipment loans Check out equipment Academic year Campus Recreation
Recreation Center Heated 10-lane pool, climbing wall, fitness equipment, and Seawolf Cyclery for free bike repairs Access with student ID Academic year Campus Recreation
Student-Parent Resources Diapers, formula, and parenting supplies for student parents Visit Basic Needs Academic year Student-Parent Resources

University of California (UC)

Jump to:

  • UC Berkeley
  • UC College of the Law, San Francisco
  • UC San Francisco (UCSF)

UC Berkeley

Program Benefit How to Use It Timeframe Link
AC Transit Free Pass Free unlimited East Bay transit on AC Transit Receive bus pass Academic year Transportation
ASUC Tax Services Free tax preparation for students earning $67,000 or less, including international students (F-1 and J-1) Schedule appointment through ASUC Legal Clinic Tax season Tax Services
BAMPFA Free Admission Free admission to Berkeley Art Museum and Pacific Film Archive with one guest Show student ID Academic year BAMPFA
Basic Needs Food Pantry Free groceries with weekly visits allowed, no questions asked Visit pantry with student ID Academic year Basic Needs
BayPass $229 per semester for unlimited rides on all 24 Bay Area transit agencies (BART, Caltrain, Muni, AC Transit, VTA, ferries, and more) Auto-loaded on student Clipper card Fall and Spring semesters BayPass
CAPS Mental Health Free mental health counseling including Lets Talk drop-in sessions and 15-minute massage chair appointments Schedule through CAPS Academic year CAPS
CHAOS Outdoor Gear Camping gear rentals for $10 annual membership through student-run club operating since 1870 Join CHAOS and rent equipment Academic year CHAOS
Free Museums & Gardens Free admission to Lawrence Hall of Science, UC Botanical Garden, and Hearst Museum of Anthropology Show student ID Academic year Campus Life
Free Printing 200 free pages per semester from Open Computing Facility (20 pages per day limit) Use OCF printers Per semester Student Tech
Free Software Free Adobe Creative Cloud, Microsoft 365, MATLAB, SPSS, Stata, and more Download or log in online Academic year UCB Software
Recreation Facilities Nearly 20 facilities including 41-foot climbing wall with free equipment and instruction Access with student ID Academic year RecWell
Student Legal Services Free legal guidance on tenant rights, contracts, consumer matters, and more Schedule appointment online Academic year SLS
UC Aquatic Center Free kayak and stand-up paddleboard rentals (up to 2.5 hours) at Berkeley Marina Reserve online with student ID Academic year Adventures

UC College of the Law, San Francisco

(formerly UC Hastings)

Program Benefit How to Use It Timeframe Link
Free Legal Clinics More than 15 legal clinics including Workers Rights, Immigrants Rights, and Startup Legal Garage Enroll in clinic courses Academic year Clinical Programs
Guaranteed Student Housing Guaranteed furnished housing at Academe at 198 building with below-market San Francisco rates Apply for housing Academic year Housing
Merit Scholarships About 80 percent of admitted students receive merit-based scholarships or need-based grants Apply for admission Upon admission Financial Aid
Public Interest Career Assistance PICAP supports graduates in public interest careers with loan repayment for up to 10 years Apply upon graduation Up to 10 years post graduation PICAP
UC Recreation Access Access to UCSFs fitness centers as UC students Show student ID Academic year Fitness

UC San Francisco (UCSF)

Program Benefit How to Use It Timeframe Link
Bakar & Millberry Fitness Centers Discounted access to rooftop pool at Mission Bay, gymnasium, and three-story climbing wall Access with student ID Academic year Fitness Centers
CalFresh & Benefits Support Application assistance for income-eligible food assistance and other public benefits Contact Basic Needs office Academic year Basic Needs
Childcare Reimbursement Eligible Academic Student Employee (ASE) and/or Graduate Student Researcher (GSR) students can receive up to $1,400 per quarter in childcare reimbursement; all students receive 10 days of free Bright Horizons back-up care annually Apply through student services Academic year UCSF Childcare Reimbursement Program
Clipper BayPass Unlimited rides on all 24 Bay Area transit agencies for a $272 per year fee that is automatically charged (worth more than $2,000 annually) Receive Clipper card Academic year Campus Life Services
Emergency Loans & Rapid Rehousing Emergency funding for students with a financial hardship due to unexpected events (up to $2K) Apply through Financial Aid As needed Emergency Funding
Food 4 UCSF Alerts Text alerts when free food is available after catered campus events Download app Academic year Food 4 UCSF (MyAccess login required)
Free Campus Shuttles Free shuttles connecting Parnassus, Mission Bay, and Zuckerberg San Francisco General Board with student ID Academic year Campus Life Services
Late Night Lyft Program UCSF covers first $10 of Lyft rides home between 8 PM and 1 AM, up to 15 rides per month (up to $150 monthly value) Use Lyft with UCSF email account Academic year Campus Life Services
Student Food Market Free fresh produce from SF Marin Food Bank every Thursday at both campuses Visit market Academic year Basic Needs
The Pantry 24/7 emergency food access for students Access with student ID Academic year Basic Needs

Program Benefit How to Use It Timeframe Link
Free Campus Shuttles Free shuttles connecting all university buildings across San Francisco Board with student ID Academic year Transportation
Gym Facilities Free access to basketball courts and skateboard ramp Access with student ID Academic year Fitness & Recreation
Transfer Scholarships Up to $10,000 over 8 semesters Apply as transfer student 8 semesters Scholarships

California College of the Arts

Program Benefit How to Use It Timeframe Link
CAPS Counseling Up to 11 free therapy sessions per semester plus 24/7 crisis hotline Schedule through CAPS Per semester CAPS
CCA Pass Unlimited Muni access for $155 per semester. Shuttle connects to BART Purchase pass Academic semester CCA Pass
Student Emergency Fund Financial support for hardship including grocery and art supplies Apply through Student Services Academic year Emergency Fund
Visionary Practice Scholarship Full tuition scholarship Apply for scholarship Academic year Financial Aid

Dominican University of California

Program Benefit How to Use It Timeframe Link
Close The Gap Scholarship Helps reduce out of pocket college costs Apply for admission Academic year Financial Aid
Conlan Center Free access to fitness center Access with student ID Academic year Recreation
Rec Sports Free club sports and outdoor recreation opportunities Join activities Academic year Recreation
TimelyCare Free 24/7 virtual medical and mental health services Access through student portal Academic year TimelyCare

Golden Gate University

Program Benefit How to Use It Timeframe Link
Community Tuition Assistance Tuition benefits for employees and families Apply through HR Academic year Tuition Assistance
Free Tutoring Tutoring for math, writing, finance, accounting and more Schedule tutoring session Academic year Student Services
International Student Scholarships Scholarships up to $15,000 for international students Apply as international student Academic year Scholarships
TimelyCare 24/7 healthcare access including urgent care and counseling Access through student portal Academic year Student Services
Yellow Ribbon Program Education benefits for eligible veterans Apply as eligible veteran Academic year Military Benefits

Saint Marys College of California

Program Benefit How to Use It Timeframe Link
Essential Needs Program Financial and food support. Housing assistance for all students Contact Community Life Academic year Essential Needs
GaelPantry Points based pantry for groceries Visit GaelPantry Academic year GaelPantry
Joseph L Alioto Recreation Center Pool and climbing wall access included with enrollment Access with student ID Academic year JARC

Samuel Merritt University

Program Benefit How to Use It Timeframe Link
Academic Support Free 24/7 Brainfuse tutoring and academic coaching Access through student portal Ongoing Academic Support
Alumni Scholarship 20% tuition discount for returning alumni Apply as returning alumnus Academic year Financial Aid
Canopy Learn Medical Spanish Free online clinical Spanish courses Access through library Ongoing Library Services
Emergency Assistance Fund Emergency funding for rent, food, transportation and more Complete the Emergency Scholarships form by clicking on “CL Scholarship Applications” in your MySMU portal As needed Student Affairs
SMU Cares Food Pantries Food support at all campus locations Visit pantry with student ID Academic year Student Affairs
Sutter Health Employee Scholarship 20% tuition discount for Sutter Health employees enrolled in College of Nursing programs Email a copy of your Sutter employee ID to your financial aid counselor Academic year Financial Aid

Santa Clara University

Program Benefit How to Use It Timeframe Link
Bronco Food Pantry Trust-based system—all requests honored without documentation of financial need; fresh produce from SCU’s Forge Garden plus donated gift cards and dining points Visit pantry Academic year Basic Needs
Clipper START Support Transportation support helps students apply for Clipper START (50% discount on BART, Muni, Caltrain) Contact transportation services Academic year Basic Needs
Ivancovich Family Student Life Fund One-time grants for unexpected emergencies Apply through Student Life As needed Basic Needs
Pat Malley Fitness Center 45,000 sq ft fitness center and Sullivan Aquatic Center: 20+ free group fitness classes weekly; summer fee waivers for Pell Grant recipients Access with student ID Academic year Recreation
Presidential at Entry Scholarship Full tuition scholarship Apply for admission Academic year Financial Aid
SCU California Promise Promise program scholarship Apply for admission Academic year Financial Aid
Therapists in Residence Embedded therapists directly in residence halls making mental health support more accessible; includes CAPS services + 24/7 crisis support Contact therapist in residence Academic year BeWell
Third-Child Grant 50% tuition when three family members enrolled simultaneously Apply with enrollment verification Academic year Financial Aid

Stanford University

Program Benefit How to Use It Timeframe Link
ASSU Legal Counseling Free legal advice for students, spouses, partners on landlord/tenant, identity theft, contracts, small claims Schedule appointment Academic year ASSU Legal Counsel Office
CAPS Mental Health Free mental health counseling supplemented by TimelyCare (24/7 virtual care + 12 scheduled therapy sessions annually) Access through CAPS or TimelyCare Academic year CAPS
Hub Tech Desk Lending Loans laptops, iPads, cameras, recording equipment to students with SUID cards Check out from Hub Academic year The Hub
Makerspace (create:space) 3D printing and crafting equipment Visit makerspace Academic year The Hub
Recreation Facilities Nearly 20 facilities: three recreation centers, aquatic center, largest collegiate climbing walls (free belay classes, $2 shoe rentals) Access with student ID Academic year Recreation
Second Harvest Pop-Ups Free produce, dairy, and proteins distributed monthly Attend pop-up distribution Monthly Student Resources
Stanford Outdoor Center (AOERC) Equipment rentals at reduced rates: tents, backpacks, sleeping bags, kayaks, surfboards, climbing gear, ski equipment Rent equipment Academic year Equipment Rentals

University of San Francisco

Program Benefit How to Use It Timeframe Link
Koret Health & Recreation Center 50-meter pool, weight room, group fitness classes at no additional cost Access with student ID Academic year Koret
Student Laptop Program Technology access through Basic Needs Program Apply through Basic Needs Academic year Student Services
Unlimited Muni Pass Free access to all SF Muni buses, streetcars, and metro through MuniMobile (worth ~$324+/semester) Activate through MuniMobile Academic year Muni Pass
USF Food Pantry Addresses food insecurity affecting ~30% of students Visit Gleeson Library pantry Academic year Food Pantry

Always verify benefits and eligibility with the service provider or your college/university as programs and requirements may change.

Community-maintained • Open Source • Free Forever
